bias_on_spontaneous: Post-July-2023 regulatory/media attention drove a 6.8x surge; stimulated reporting raises the numerator without any change in true risk. Full-DB ROR unaffected only because the whole DB rose too.

metric: 471/912 for obesity or T2D; 104/912 adjudicated indication-confounded
bias_on_spontaneous: Obesity and T2D both carry elevated baseline depression/suicide risk. Against a general-population background the drug inherits its indication's risk; the SGLT2i/DPP-4i comparator partly shares T2D but not obesity, explaining part of the 0.77->2.08 flip.
Not a bias in the data but in the analysis: the entire positive signal exists only under a same-indication comparator. This is the single largest driver of the headline number.
Nearly half are consumer/non-HP reports, which carry less clinical detail and higher susceptibility to media priming; over-represent subjective outcomes like ideation vs adjudicated attempts.
16.8% co-report an antidepressant and 7.8% a benzodiazepine, marking pre-existing psychiatric morbidity — a competing cause of the outcome not attributable to the GLP-1 RA.

metric: unknown-indication 287/912; duplicate flag not de-duplicated by openFDA
bias_on_spontaneous: openFDA does not collapse overlapping case versions; a single patient reported by consumer + clinician + manufacturer inflates counts. 31% have no usable indication, limiting adjustment.
